rationale: The signals show heavy search demand around morph identification, breeding setup, and specific morphs (Cappuccino, Lilly White, Axanthic), but almost entirely driven by Google autocomplete and YouTube—most of which covers general care, identification, and sales intent. No forum heat, no Bluesky engagement, no rising trends. The breeder blog is promotional only. This is a low-signal environment: the audience is searching for basic info and product deals, not seeking expert breeding strategy or genetics insight. I'm rejecting all topics because the signals lack the specificity (forum confusion, disagreement, or a concrete misconception) needed to justify a breeding-focused blog post. The queued slugs already cover Lilly White ID and Axanthic breeding; YouTube content dominates the morph-education space; and general care is outside our remit.
